title: Electrophoretic transfer of RNA to nylon membrane (Semi-dry blotting)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4genn","text":"Protocol for transfer of RNA to nylon membrane for subsequent Northern Blot analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Separate RNA in a gel. Denaturing RNA Urea-PAGE by Anna Behle

Prepare the following materials 8 layers of 3MM whatman papers (1x gel size) Hybond N+ Nylon membrane (1x gel size) Soak in 0.5 x TBE buffer. Gel should also be incubated in 0.5 x TBE prior to electrophoretic transfer.

Transfer is set up in a Trans-Blot SD Semi-Dry Electrophoretic Transfer Cell (BioRad). Prior to assembly, humidify both electrode plates with 0.5 x TBE buffer. From the bottom up, stack the blotting sandwich as follows: 4 layers of 3 MM whatman papers Nylon membrane PAA gel → Remove all bubbles with a glas bar 4 layers of 3 MM whatman papers
Close lid and plug in electrodes. Run at constant current of 2 mA/cm 2 gel for 45 minutes. 00:45:00
creator: Anna Behle
